как добавить сниппет в vs code? для Java System.out.println

Чтобы добавить сниппет в VS Code для Java System.out.println, вы можете выполнить следующие шаги:

1. Откройте VS Code и перейдите к настройкам. Вы можете сделать это с помощью горячих клавиш Ctrl+,
2. Введите "snippets" в поле поиска настроек и выберите "Пользовательские файлы Snippets" (Preferences: Open User Snippets),
3. Выберите язык "Java",
4. В файле сниппетов Java добавьте следующий код:

```json
"Print Line": {
   "prefix": "sout",
   "body": [
       "System.out.println($1);"
   ],
   "description": "System.out.println snippet"
}
```
5. Сохраните файл сниппетов (например, назовите его "java.json").

Теперь, когда вы вводите "sout" в файле Java в VS Code и нажимаете Tab, сниппет "System.out.println" будет автоматически добавлен.

Надеюсь, это поможет вам добавить сниппет для System.out.println в VS Code для Java!
как писать в консоли по русски на Java

В Java можно написать на русском языке в консоли, но для этого требуется правильно настроить кодировку. Вот несколько шагов, которые помогут вам написать на русском языке в консоли на Java:

1. Установите кодировку консоли:

```java
System.setOut(new PrintStream(System.out, true, "UTF-8")); 
```

2. Установите кодировку для ввода с клавиатуры:

```java
Scanner scanner = new Scanner(System.in, "UTF-8");
```

3. Убедитесь, что ваш файл Java (*.java) сохранен в кодировке UTF-8. 

4. Установите кодировку для текстовых ресурсов, которые вы хотите вывести на русском языке:

```java
String message = "Привет, мир!";
System.out.println(message);
```

5. Убедитесь, что вашу консоль/терминал правильно настроена для отображения символов в кодировке UTF-8.

Обратите внимание, что эти шаги должны быть выполнены до того, как вы начнете выводить или вводить русский текст.

is there a way to setup VS Code so that when I press Ctrl+arrow it navigates words in camelCase? instead of jumping to beginning or end of the identifier

Yes, you can set up VS Code to navigate words in camelCase when pressing Ctrl+arrow keys. To do this, you can modify the keybindings in VS Code.

Follow these steps:

1. Open VS Code and go to "File" > "Preferences" > "Keyboard Shortcuts" (or use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+K Ctrl+S).
2. In the Keyboard Shortcuts tab, on the right-hand side, you will see a file called "keybindings.json". Click on it.
3. In the keybindings.json file, add the following code:

```json
[
    {
        "key": "ctrl+right",
        "command": "cursorWordPartRight",
        "when": "editorTextFocus && !editorHasSelection && camelCaseWordPartNavigation.enabled"
    },
    {
        "key": "ctrl+left",
        "command": "cursorWordPartLeft",
        "when": "editorTextFocus && !editorHasSelection && camelCaseWordPartNavigation.enabled"
    }
]
```
4. Save the file (Ctrl+S).

Now, when you press Ctrl+right arrow, it will navigate to the next camelCase word part, and when you press Ctrl+left arrow, it will navigate to the previous camelCase word part.

Note: If the keybindings.json file was empty initially, make sure to enclose the added code within square brackets [].

Hope this helps!
doesn't work. how to set camelCaseWordPartNavigation enabled?

I apologize for the confusion. It seems that the `camelCaseWordPartNavigation` feature is not available in the default keybindings of VS Code.

To enable camel case word part navigation, you can install the "Camel Case Navigation" extension from the VS Code marketplace. Here's how:

1. Open VS Code
2. Click on the Extensions icon in the left sidebar (or use the shortcut Ctrl+Shift+X).
3. Search for "Camel Case Navigation" in the marketplace search bar.
4. Click on the extension offered by "Luis Alejandro Peña" and install it.
5. Once the extension is installed, you should be able to navigate words in camel case using Ctrl+arrow keys.

Please note that after installing the extension, you may need to reload VS Code for the changes to take effect.

I apologize for any confusion caused and hope this solution works for you.
You're welcome! I'm glad I could help. If you have any more questions, feel free to ask. Happy coding!
is there a internal java function that returns index of item in String array?
what exception to throw if array index goes out of bounds?

In Java, if an array index goes out of bounds, you typically throw an `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`.

`ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` is a runtime exception that occurs when you try to access an array element using an index that is either negative or exceeds the array's length.

Here's an example of how you can throw an `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`:

```java
if (index < 0 || index >= array.length) {
    throw new ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException("Array index is out of bounds");
}
```

In the example above, if the `index` is less than 0 or greater than or equal to the length of the array, an `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` is thrown with a corresponding message.

You can catch and handle this exception using a try-catch block, or let it propagate up the call stack if you choose not to handle it explicitly.

It's important to note that `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` is a runtime exception, which means it does not need to be declared in the method signature or handled explicitly (unless you want to).
